Impact skew summary

▼

VIII. Open Analysis Questions

Deferred questions (Q18-Q23)

Note: These require additional data fetches from Overton, PDF downloads, or comparison-funder data.

#QuestionWhy we cannot answer it nowWhat ingest is needed
18Citation impact of each policy doc itself (does the policy doc get cited downstream?)Overton tracks policy->policy citations but we have not pulled them.Re-fetch each policy doc with Overton's downstream citation field; cache locally.
19Prominence of IES research within the policy doc (passing mention vs central evidence)We only have whether the citation exists, not where it appears in the text.Download the 3,424 PDFs, extract text, run section detection (Lit Review vs Recommendations).
20Causal chain: did the IES research change the policy?Causal counterfactual not directly observable.Annotate a sample of policy docs where IES is cited in the Recommendations section vs only the literature review; manual coding by a research assistant for ~100 docs.
21Baseline: are 3,424 policy citations a lot or a little?Need a comparison universe (NSF education, Gates/Spencer, NIH).Run the same Overton-based pipeline on a comparison funder's grants; or pull Overton totals for the topic to compute IES share-of-voice.
22Federal-agency split within the 1,360 USA federal/national docspolicy_source_title is sparse for federal docs (918 NA out of 1,471 USA).For each USA doc with NA source_title, parse the policy_document_url or title to identify agency (DoEd / HHS / NSF / OECD-affiliated etc.).
23Author-affiliation analysis (do IES PIs themselves write the policy docs?)policy_authors field is broken (placeholder string for 3,262 of 3,424 docs).Re-fetch authors via Overton API; alternatively scrape from the policy_document_url for each doc.

Full analysis question backlog (Q1-Q23)

Note: Complete list of questions identified at the start of this analytical pillar.

ThemeQuestion
VolumeHow has the policy-doc citation count of IES research grown by year? Is there an inflection point?
VolumeWhat share of IES grants ever land in any policy doc, and how does this vary by award cohort?
GeographyWhich non-US countries cite IES research the most relative to their education-policy publishing volume? (need a denominator from Overton totals)
GeographyWhich US states publish the most IES-derived policy docs? Are they the same states funding K-12 reforms?
GeographyHow does the federal vs state mix of US policy docs compare to the federal vs state mix of *non-IES* education policy docs (a baseline)?
GeographyAre there continents we are missing entirely (Africa, South America), or is that a true reflection of where IES research is being adopted?
Source typeWithin think tanks, which 5 organizations dominate, and do they cluster on certain IES topic areas (e.g., RAND on military families, Brookings on equity)?
Source typeWhat share of IES research enters policy via direct citation vs. via an intermediate meta-analysis? Does the share differ by country?
TimingMedian grant->policy lag (years) by country. Are some regions implementing faster?
TimingWithin the US, does federal policy adoption lag or lead state-level adoption?
TimingMedian meta->policy lag by country, holding meta-analysis constant — does the same meta paper reach different countries at different speeds?
Topical fitWhich IES topic areas (Reading, Math, Special Education, Workforce, etc.) have the broadest international reach?
Topical fitAre NCSER (special-ed) grants more likely to reach US state policy than NCER grants? (institutional fit hypothesis)
Top performersWhich 20 IES grants have produced the most distinct policy-doc citations? What do they share (institution, PI, topic, scale-up vs efficacy)?
Top performersWhich IES papers (single DOIs) appear in the most distinct policy docs? Independent of grant.
Influence pathFor meta-analysis-mediated reach, which meta-analyses are the bottleneck — i.e., a small number of metas account for most international reach?
Influence pathAre there policy docs that cite many IES grants together? If so, those are 'synthesis docs' worth flagging.
UnderusedWhich grants have many publications but zero policy reach? Are they recent (lag) or genuinely under-utilized?
UnderusedWhich countries cite an IES paper but never the underlying grant — i.e., the research traveled but the funding attribution didn't?
Quality controlAre there policy_document_ids with suspicious metadata (no date, no country, no title) that should be flagged for re-fetch?
NetworkBuild a co-citation network: which pairs of IES grants are cited in the same policy doc? Look for clusters.
EquityAre minority-serving-institution grants under- or over-represented in policy adoption relative to PWI grants?
RecencyRestrict to policy docs published in the last 5 years — does the geographic mix shift compared to all-time?
IES Grant-to-Policy Pipeline • Stages 12–13e • May 05, 2026
